item.text: The state reads are still returning. If the confirmed lead win is active, I’ll record it through the strict commit path instead of playing past a terminal event.

P2 did not clear the unit-count lead: P1 starts turn 013 at 7 units to P2’s 2 after having created the pending lead on turn 011. I’m checking the commit/validation schema for how this runner records terminal start-of-turn wins so I can stop at the legal win event rather than continue play.

The runner still requires deck and board result files for a committed entry, so I’m going to make the smallest legal turn artifacts while recording the confirmed start-of-turn unit-lead event. That avoids playing past the win more than the tooling requires.
The minimal artifacts and confirmed win event are written. I’m running deck and board resolution now, starting with the no-action deck turn.
The deck command is still running, so I’m polling it before moving on. If the CLI requires more explicit buy-phase behavior, I’ll adjust the deck file.
The minimal deck result was accepted. I’m running the empty board action file next; this will still let the CLI apply its standard board-phase bookkeeping.
Board resolution succeeded. I’m inspecting the result briefly, then I’ll commit with the confirmed `unitLead` event under strict win validation.
The result is intentionally minimal: no cards played or bought, no board actions, with the win represented in the event file. I’m committing now with strict win validation.
